name_it: Saint Ignatius of Antioch, bishop and martyr
name_en: Saint Ignatius of Antioch, bishop and martyr
name_es: Saint Ignatius of Antioch, bishop and martyr
name_pt: Saint Ignatius of Antioch, bishop and martyr
bio_en: Ignatius of Antioch, also known as Ignatius Theophorus, was an early Christian writer and the Patriarch of Antioch. While en route to Rome, where he was to be killed, Ignatius wrote a series of letters. This correspondence forms a central part of a later collection of works by the Apostolic Fathers. He is considered one of the three most important of these, together with Clement of Rome and Polycarp. Although the authenticity of his letters has been questioned, they continue to serve as an example of early Christian theology, and address important topics including ecclesiology, the sacraments, and the role of bishops.
